name: Telegram Mini App runtime hardening and bundle reduction
description: Harden the TMA startup/runtime path, auth-storage fallback behavior, safe-area reactivity, and route loading so the Mini App launches reliably and loads less code on cold start.

planned: Catch Telegram SDK launch failures before React render and route them into a renderable fatal state.
Make TMA safe-area CSS vars reactive after viewport/fullscreen changes instead of sync-once behavior.
Trip auth storage permanently into memory-only mode after secure read/delete failures and simplify bootstrap dead branches.
Lazy-load route modules to reduce the current eager TMA startup bundle.
Collapse duplicated lightweight helpers only after runtime/auth correctness fixes land.
artifacts: Added `initTelegramSafely()` and threaded startup failure handling through `apps/tma/src/main.tsx` and `apps/tma/src/app/app.tsx` so missing Telegram launch params now render the existing fatal launch UI instead of crashing before React mounts.
Reworked `apps/tma/src/lib/telegram/theme.ts` to bind `miniApp`, `themeParams`, and `viewport` CSS vars once, keep safe-area vars reactive via `viewport.bindCssVars()`, and clean them up on teardown.
Hardened `apps/tma/src/lib/storage/adapter.ts` so secure read/delete failures now trip the adapter into explicit memory-only mode instead of silently retrying broken SecureStorage calls forever.

Removed currently unreachable bootstrap branches in `apps/tma/src/features/auth/bootstrap.tsx`, `apps/tma/src/features/auth/bootstrap-deps.ts`, and `apps/tma/src/features/auth/store.ts` without changing the existing authenticated/fatal outcomes.

Lazy-loaded the TMA route modules in `apps/tma/src/app/router/app-router.tsx`, removed the duplicate explicit `/home` route, and preserved the same page components behind `Suspense` + `LoadingFallback`.

Preserved create-household partial-failure feedback by carrying avatar-patch errors through router state into the household detail page instead of setting feedback immediately before navigation and losing it on unmount.
verification: Focused TMA verification passed on 2026-06-04: `pnpm --filter tma exec vitest run src/test/home-presentation.test.ts src/test/storage-adapter.test.ts src/test/theme.test.ts src/test/telegram-init.test.ts src/test/auth-bootstrap.test.ts src/test/safe-area.test.ts` passed with 24 tests across 6 files.

Focused TMA build verification passed on 2026-06-04: `pnpm --filter tma build` succeeded after lazy routes and formatter caching, emitting split route chunks and reducing the eager entry bundle to `436.00 kB` minified / `139.30 kB` gzip.
Repo canonical verification passed on 2026-06-04: `./init.sh lint` returned `OK`, `./init.sh build` returned `OK`, and final `./init.sh` completed with `Done!`.
Repo hygiene checks passed on 2026-06-04: `git diff --check` returned clean.

Final `gitnexus_detect_changes(scope: 'all', repo: 'household-finance-system')` reported `critical` whole-worktree risk with 16 changed files / 95 changed symbols / 20 affected processes because this milestone touches startup, auth, router, and shared presentation surfaces together.

notes: This feature is a hardening and architecture pass over the active TMA worktree. It intentionally prioritizes runtime correctness and cold-start behavior over new product scope.
